questions.text: Which of these substances tastes sour?
questions.explanation: Acids are chemical substances that have a sour taste.

questions.text: What color does blue litmus paper turn when dipped in an acid?
questions.explanation: Acids turn blue litmus paper red.
Substances that are bitter in taste and soapy to touch are known as bases.

questions.text: Which indicator turns green in a basic solution?
questions.explanation: China rose indicator turns acidic solutions dark pink and basic solutions green.

questions.text: The reaction between an acid and a base is called:
questions.explanation: Neutralization results in the formation of salt and water with the evolution of heat.

questions.text: Which acid is present in an ant's sting?
questions.explanation: An ant injects formic acid into the skin when it bites.

questions.text: Milk of Magnesia contains which base?
questions.explanation: Magnesium hydroxide is used as an antacid to neutralize excessive stomach acid.

questions.text: What happens when phenolphthalein is added to an acidic solution?
questions.explanation: Phenolphthalein remains colorless in acidic solutions and turns pink in basic solutions.

questions.text: Common salt is chemically known as:
questions.explanation: Sodium chloride (NaCl) is a neutral salt used in our food.

questions.text: Which of these is a natural indicator?
questions.explanation: Turmeric is a natural substance that changes color in the presence of a base.
